rule.desc: Use type aliases for complex types. Use `TypeAlias` annotation for clarity.
rule.rationale: Type aliases improve readability, reduce duplication, and centralize type definitions.

example.code:
# Good - clear and reusable TransactionList: TypeAlias = list[Transaction] ConfigDict: TypeAlias = dict[str, str | int | bool] ParseResult: TypeAlias = tuple[bool, TransactionList | None, str | None]
def parse_file(path: str) -> ParseResult: """Returns (success, transactions, error_message).""" ...
